REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT DAVID LEUNG, FENG CHEN NOTICE OF MOTION AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, AS ALLEGED ARISES OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY, WHICH IS ALSO SUBJECT TO CC 47 PRIVILEGE: (A) FALSE CLAIMS OF NUISANCE CONTAINED IN THE NOTICE AND MALICIOUS PROSECUTION AGAINST PLAINTIFFS, UNLAWFUL DETAINER AND CIVIL HARASSMENT. FAC 7:7-8; 4:21-22; 4:24-5:19; 5:20-22. THEREFORE DEFENDANTS SHIFTED THEIR BURDEN ON THE SECOND PRONG AND PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO SUSTAIN THEIR BURDEN UNDER SECOND PRONG. MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. THIS IS A MIXED CAUSE OF ACTION AND PS HAVE SHOWN THAT THEIR NON-PROTECTED ACTIVITY PART OF THE CLAIM HAS "MINIMAL MERIT." SEE WALLACE V. MCCUBBIN (2011) 196 CAL.APP.4TH 1169. PLAINTIFF PROVIDED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE RE: VIOLATIONS OF SFRO 37.10(A)(1), (4), (10) AND (11). REQUEST FOR FEES IS DENIED. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Jul-20-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-16-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michon Spinelli, Esq. (650-780-1644) for Defendant/Moving party DAVID LEUNG, FENG CHEN.
No other Appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DAVID LEUNG, FENG CHEN Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Amended Complaint is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to the first cause of action. The first cause of action, as alleged arises out of protected activity, which is also subject to CC 47 privilege: (a) false claims of nuisance contained in the Notice and malicious prosecution against Plaintiffs, unlawful detainer and civil harassment. FAC 7:7-8; 4:21-22; 4:24-5:19; 5:20-22. Therefore Defendants shifted their burden on the second prong and Plaintiffs failed to sustain their burden under second prong. Motion is DENIED as to the second cause of action. This is a mixed cause of action and Ps have shown that their non-protected activity part of the claim has "minimal merit." See Wallace v. McCubbin (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 1169. Plaintiff provided sufficient evidence re: violations of SFRO 37.10(a)(1), (4), (10) and (11). Request for fees is DENIED. Order is signed in open court. (see scanned order for details)Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)
